description: Public opinion in Brazil during the implementation of the Plano Real, designed to end hyperinflation by introducing a new currency, the Real. The policy faced initial skepticism from a population exhausted by failed past stabilization attempts.

key_events.description: Finance Minister FHC launches the URV as a transitional unit.
Introduction of the Real currency causes immediate drop in prices.
Hyperinflation drops below 10% monthly, creating mass popularity.
FHC wins the 1994 presidential election in the first round.

calibration_notes: Excellent case study for how rapid visible results can flip public sentiment from deep cynicism to high support.
